methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $104M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Alice & Bob is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $104M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader universe of 11 quantum computing peers—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum—though narrow stage and valuation-tier alignment constraints limited peer-median applicability.

Stage-progression methodology was the primary applicable approach, using total capital raised ($104M) as a base value proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement. This yielded an implied valuation of $198M. A sensitivity analysis around this step-up multiplier—testing ±0.15x variation—produced a range of $168M (low) to $227M (high), establishing a median point at $198M.

Peer-median triangulation could not be reliably applied due to insufficient datapoints meeting narrow filters (same-stage and adjacent-stage quantum companies with comparable valuation tier). While 7 same-sector peers exist broadly, the sample lacked the minimum 3 comparable points needed for statistical confidence. The analysis therefore relies on a single method, introducing material uncertainty around any point estimate. Key risks include stage-progression assumptions not reflecting quantum sector-specific fundraising dynamics, and limited visibility into comparable company trading multiples or post-Series B financing patterns. A valuation range of $168M–$227M is supported, with $198M as the median expectation, pending broader data on peer financings.
